front: Novel: the matriarch Aunt Ada Doom repeatedly claims she once 'saw something nasty in the woodshed'
Novel: a pompous writer named Mr. Mybug works on a biography claiming Branwell was the true genius of the Bronte siblings
Novel: Cousin Amos preaches to the Church of the Quivering Brethren that 'there'll be no butter in Hell'
Novel: especially turgid passages are preceded by Baedeker-style star ratings of their prose quality
Novel: set in the fictional Sussex village of Howling, where the protagonist stays with the Starkadder family
Novel: parody of the 'loam and lovechild' rural fiction tradition by authors like Sheila Kaye-Smith and Mary Webb
Novel: the farmhand Adam Lambsbreath uses twigs for 'clettering' and is too proud of the mop the protagonist buys him
Novel: farm residents bear nicknames including Aimless, Feckless, Graceless, Pointless, and Big Business
Novel: the American movie producer Earl Neck turns the protagonist's cousin Seth into the star of 'Small-Town Sheik'
Novel: the protagonist says her idea of hell is 'a large party in a cold room where everybody has to play hockey properly'
Novel: rooms in the title property are labeled 'Quiete Retreate' and 'Greate laundrie'
Novel: the protagonist draws strength from The Pensees of the Abbe Fausse-Maigre
Novel: may be a parody of John Cowper Powys's Wolf Solent
Novel: at a screening of the Norwegian-Japanese film 'Yes,' the protagonist meets the American movie producer Earl Neck
